claimText: The Namami Gange Programme is implemented by the National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) and uses the river basin as its unit of planning.
evidenceExcerpt: The Namami Gange Programme is implemented by the National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG), using the river basin as the unit of planning.
examUse: Statement-based question on the implementation and planning unit of the Namami Gange Programme.
distractorHooks: Uses the state or district as the unit of planning
Implemented directly by the Ministry of Jal Shakti without a dedicated mission

claimText: The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) was registered as a society under the Societies Registration Act, 1860.
evidenceExcerpt: NMCG was registered as a society in 2011 under the Societies Registration Act, 1860.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal status of NMCG.
distractorHooks: Statutory body created under the Water Act, 1974

claimText: The National Ganga River Basin Authority (NGRBA) was constituted under the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
evidenceExcerpt: NGRBA was constituted under the Environment Protection Act, 1986
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the parent legislation of NGRBA.
distractorHooks: Constituted under the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974

claimText: The National River Conservation Plan (NRCP) is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme that covers identified river stretches but excludes the Ganga basin.
evidenceExcerpt: The National River Conservation Plan (NRCP) is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme covering identified river stretches excluding the Ganga basin.
examUse: Statement-based question on the coverage and funding pattern of the NRCP.
distractorHooks: Includes the Ganga basin in its coverage
